name: Unity WebGL Game
description: Mount Unity WebGL games using the Livewall standard: persistent layout-level context, version.json resolution, platform detection, and structured boot sequence
notes: IMPORTANT: Wrap {children} in the campaign layout with <UnityContainer> so Unity persists across routes (video → gameplay → result).
Unity build must include LivewallBridge.jslib — copy public/LivewallBridge.jslib to Assets/Plugins/WebGL/ in your Unity project.
Unity build must emit a version.json at {NEXT_PUBLIC_UNITY_BASE_URL}/{NEXT_PUBLIC_UNITY_GAME_NAME}/version.json with { currentVersion: N }.
Unity build files must live at {baseUrl}/{gameName}/V{N}/{platform}/ where platform is 'desktop', 'ios', or 'android'.
Boot sequence: initializeUnity (createUnityInstance → sceneLoaded) → preloadScene (SetScene → addressableLoaded) → loadScene (LoadScene → sceneLoaded) → startGame (StartGame).
Call ctx.setData({ translations, playTutorial }) before fullBoot — UnityGame adds environment + muted automatically.
For replay support call ctx.replayBoot() instead of fullBoot() on subsequent plays.
See components/_modules/unity/IUnity.ts to extend IUnitySetData with campaign-specific fields.
